$comment: Indexed char byte memory (D-CSUBSET-CHAR-STRING-VALUE-CODEGEN + D-AS4-ARM64-BASE-INDEX-LEA): runtime-index byte STORE `q[n]=66` (x86 mov [base+idx*1],r8 / arm64 STURB) + indexed byte LOAD `q[n]` (movzx / LDURB), through (char*)&int writable stack memory. 66 + 65 = 131. The base+index address (`q+n`) lowers to a 4-op `lea` (x86 SIB / arm64 `ADD Xd,Xn,Xm`); arm64 gained that base+index lea form this cycle (the universal memoffset.zero slot), so the example now runs on EVERY target — it was x86-only ONLY because arm64's lea was symbol+disp with no base+index form. The optimizedPipelines arm keeps `n` a runtime value (no const-fold of the index).
